pages.full_text:
In the morning we walked into Bad Honnef + the boys spent their pocket money. Annie took us up the cog railway in the afternoon to the of the "[?7n] Hills", the Dragonsfeld. A million people a year visit it. Lovely views of the Rhine up to Bonn, + beyond to Cologne on a clear day. We walked down.

__Thursday__
    We walked along the Rhein, crossed [to an] island + took 2 ferries with Patrick. André was at school + [F]ather stayed at home to wait with Annie. [I]'ve bought some Birkenstock sandals at the factory on the way back to the Wegners' house, while the boys played in a huge tub of balls.
    After lunch we set off for Cala[is]
pages.notes: The 'cog railway' top-of-hills destination is written as 'Dragonsfeld' — likely the Drachenfels near Bad Honnef/Königswinter on the Rhine. The heading 'Thursday' is underlined. The phrase 'of the "[?7n] Hills"' is partially obscured; could read 'the Seven Hills' (referring to the Siebengebirge). 'Patrick' and 'André' appear to be local contacts (the Wegners). The year is not stated on this page; Friday 10th August on the right page suggests 1990 (August 10 was a Friday in 1990). Bottom of page is cut off mid-sentence continuing onto right page.

The word 'Calais' is underlined in the text. 'Friday 10th August' — August 10 fell on a Friday in 1990, consistent with the volume range (1989–2003). Year not explicitly written on the page; inferred from day-date match. Text continues off the bottom of the page (sentence ends with a semicolon, suggesting more follows on the next page). 'Simon' appears to be the diarist's partner/husband.
